title: RStack attestation envelope (attestations/*.attestation.json)
description: DSSE-style envelope written by attestRun (src/core/harness/attestations.js, #73) at .rstack/runs/<run_id>/attestations/. This file defines its OWN envelope (the 'schema' field) and is therefore exempt from the Kubernetes-style resource envelope — the schema documents exactly what the code writes today.

schema.description: ATTESTATION_SCHEMA — verification rejects any other value.

subject.description: WHAT was attested: the run, the task, the commit, and file checksums.

run_id.description: Must match the run being verified (mismatched-subject otherwise).

commit.description: HEAD sha at attest time; null when the project is not a git checkout.

sha256.description: Hex sha-256 at attest time; null records a path the contract CLAIMED but that did not exist.

producer.description: WHO produced the attested contract — the #72 identity fields. Every key is recorded, null when unknown.

predicateType.description: PREDICATE_TYPES — verification reports an unknown type as invalid-predicate.

predicate.description: The contract snapshot itself (builder.json / validation.json / readiness.json content at attest time).

signature.description: Signing modes are modest by design: 'unsigned' (default, checksums still detect drift) or 'local-dev-signature' (HMAC-SHA256 keyed by RSTACK_ATTESTATION_KEY). signature.type is an open extension point — the verifier reports unknown types honestly instead of pretending to verify, so the schema does not close the enum.

value.description: Empty string when unsigned; hex HMAC otherwise.
